jobs.title: Principal Architect, Enterprise Architecture
jobs.description: View All Jobs For Employers Join Log In   View All Jobs     Summary Generated by Built In FreeWheel, a Comcast company, provides comprehensive ad platforms for publishers, advertisers, and media buyers. Powered by premium video content, robust data, and advanced technology, we're making it easier for buyers and sellers to transact across all screens, data types, and sales channels. As a global company, we have offices in nine countries and can insert advertisements around the world. Job Summary Freewheel's Enterprise Architecture team partners closely with our Product and Engineering organizations to translate target business outcomes and client requirements into scalable product designs and technical solutions - delivered on our cloud-native platforms. You will be a strategic member of the architecture group that will provide recommendations and technology design guidance for Product Management and Technology leadership to drive smart investment across our technology portfolio.  We are looking for an architect with experience in the Ad Tech industry who can quickly ramp up on our business processes, application portfolio, and data architecture - in order to develop Solution Blueprints, product-aligned Architecture Roadmaps, Reference Architectures and Extensibility Frameworks that accelerate innovation while protecting performance, reliability, security, and cost efficiency. Job Description Core Responsibilities Serve as the architecture interface between Product and Engineering, converting market and client needs into pragmatic, end-to-end solution designs for Streaming Hub (SSP/ad server), Programmatic Demand (DSP), Audience/Identity, Measurement, and Revenue Science platforms. Produce Solution Blueprints, Reference Architectures, and Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) that balance speed with long-term maintainability, platform consistency, and extensibility. Guide Product-aligned Architecture Roadmaps, sequencing capabilities to deliver measurable business val

jobs.title: AI Product Portfolio Director- Marketing
jobs.description: View All Jobs For Employers Join Log In   View All Jobs     Summary Generated by Built In Company Description It all started in sunny San Diego, California in 2004 when a visionary engineer, Fred Luddy, saw the potential to transform how we work. Fast forward to today - ServiceNow stands as a global market leader, bringing innovative AI-enhanced technology to over 8,100 customers, including 85% of the Fortune 500®. Our intelligent cloud-based platform seamlessly connects people, systems, and processes to empower organizations to find smarter, faster, and better ways to work. But this is just the beginning of our journey. Join us as we pursue our purpose to make the world work better for everyone. Job Description What you get to do in this role: Want to define how AI reshapes the end-to-end marketing lifecycle and turns strategy into durable operating leverage? This role sits at the intersection of marketing, product, and enterprise AI execution. You will own the strategic spine that connects AI ambition to real productivity, portfolio coherence, and measurable growth. As AI Product Portfolio Director, you will define a singular vision for how AI is deployed across marketing, translate that vision into an actionable MarTech and agentic roadmap, and ensure investments compound rather than fragment. This is not an experimentation role. It is a Product Management role with accountability for outcomes. You will work across Global Marketing, Technology, and external partners to ensure AI capabilities align to ServiceNow's platform, product direction, and enterprise standards. Success is measured by adoption, efficiency gains, and the organization's rising AI fluency, not by pilots shipped. Key Responsibilities: Own the AI MarTech and agentic roadmap, prioritizing capabilities that eliminate redundant workflows, increase speed to market, and improve decision quality. Evaluate AI tools, platforms, and vendor offerings to determine strategic fit, scalability, and integration

jobs.title: Product Manager, Growth
jobs.description: View All Jobs For Employers Join Log In   View All Jobs     Summary Generated by Built In Who we are Samsara (NYSE: IOT) is the pioneer of the Connected Operations™ Cloud, which is a platform that enables organizations that depend on physical operations to harness Internet of Things (IoT) data to develop actionable insights and improve their operations. At Samsara, we are helping improve the safety, efficiency and sustainability of the physical operations that power our global economy. Representing more than 40% of global GDP, these industries are the infrastructure of our planet, including agriculture, construction, field services, transportation, and manufacturing — and we are excited to help digitally transform their operations at scale. Working at Samsara means you’ll help define the future of physical operations and be on a team that’s shaping an exciting array of product solutions, including Video-Based Safety, Vehicle Telematics, Apps and Driver Workflows, and Equipment Monitoring. As part of a recently public company, you’ll have the autonomy and support to make an impact as we build for the long term. About the role: The Growth Product team builds products and systems that accelerate Samsara's go-to-market engine — turning customer demand into revenue across the customer lifecycle, from acquisition through renewal. We’re looking for a Product Manager to drive revenue growth by building products that increase customer lead acquisition, expand revenue pipeline generation, and enable sellers and marketers to operate faster and more effectively. You will partner closely with Engineering, Design, Sales, Marketing, and GTM teams to lead products that influence $XXXM+ in revenue pipeline and XXXK+ leads annually. This role is a great fit if you’re passionate about growing businesses through exceptional product experiences, enjoy operating across complex systems, and thrive in environments where data, experimentation, and customer empathy guide decisions. Our team

jobs.title: Business Development Manager, CBG
jobs.description: View All Jobs For Employers Join Log In   View All Jobs     Summary Generated by Built In Toast creates technology to help restaurants and local businesses succeed in a digital world, helping business owners operate, increase sales, engage customers, and keep employees happy. After years building the best POS platform for restaurateurs, Toast is expanding its offerings into other food and beverage concepts such as convenience stores, grocery stores, and bottle shops. This role will require a founder’s mindset as we grow Toast’s presence in this new vertical with a new offering: the Toast Retail platform. This role offers a fantastic opportunity to join the sales leadership team at Toast! What you’ll do (Responsibilities): As a Manager for the Business Development team, you will hire, develop, coach, and train a team of outbound business development professionals. If you enjoy managing and building a team and are excited about transforming the way convenience stores, grocery stores, and bottle shops operate, then this is the role for you! Hire, coach and manage a team of Business Development Representatives (BDR) to exceed quota. Lead by example in demonstrating Toast’s core values and inspiring a culture of high performance, respect and fun. Manage day to day activities of the BDR team including: live coaching, career and skill development, monitor productivity metrics to identify opportunities and risks, and ultimately drive results on activity, opportunity generation and revenue creation. Keep a tight focus on outcomes, impact, and productivity, and report results to senior growth sales and marketing leadership. Work closely with cross-functional partners on the Retail Sales Team, such as managers and account executives, to ensure proper alignment on opportunity quality. jobs.title: Business Development Executive - German Speaker
jobs.description: About us: Adzuna is an established and profitable job search engine that lists every job, everywhere. Launched in 2011, we help over 10 million visitors a month in 19 markets find better, more fulfilling jobs. Governments and the world’s largest companies trust us for our unique global audience and jobs market intelligence, and we walk the walk on things like Salary Transparency and Gender Pay. The role: Adzuna’s success to date has been down to the skills, hard work and determination of the brilliant people we have hired. Right now, we're seeking a Business Development Executive to join our International team and help us take our ambitious global plans to the next level. This role offers an incredible opportunity to work on new business accounts in the international recruitment industry and take our Germanic market to the next level, making a difference by helping companies hire more of the right people with the support of a first-class team. You’ll be joining a collaborative team of 10, working closely with fellow Account Managers and cross-functionally with teams including Delivery, Marketing, and Product. While your experience matters, what truly excites us is your energy, curiosity, and commitment to excellence. We’re looking for someone who embraces challenges, brings fresh ideas to the table, and takes pride in making a real impact — both for our clients and for millions of jobseekers around the world. What’s in it for you? At Adzuna, we’re on a mission to use the power of technology to help people find better, more fulfilling jobs. As a growing scale-up with global reach, we’re at a pivotal stage in our journey — expanding rapidly, launching innovative labour market data tools, and unlocking new opportunities in international markets. It’s a truly exciting time to join us. When you join Adzuna, you’ll be part of a high-energy team that moves fast, acts like owners, and works collaboratively toward a shared goal. This is an exceptional chance to drive your pe

From our launch in the UK in 2011, we now have tens of millions of visitors a month and are busy conquering the world from our office in West London, helping millions of people find better, more fulfilling jobs. The role: We're seeking a B2B Marketing Manager to define, lead and execute our B2B strategy in 19 markets with a focus on the US and UK. You will strive for excellence, try new things, inspire and engage those around you and feel ownership of overall company success as well as pride in doing your bit. What’s in it for you? Adzuna is a growing scale-up, championing real change in the job market globally - using the power of tech to match people to better, more fulfilling jobs. jobs.title: Press - In the news
jobs.description:
Are you based in India? Select your country to see jobs specific to your location. Adzuna is a global leader in job market statistics and insights. Our real-time data
                is used and trusted by
                the Department for Work and Pensions , the Cabinet Office and the Office
                    for National Statistics . We can work with you to provide unique insights for your media stories, including: See more about our Labour Market Intelligence tools and services The Startup on a mission to Restart Britain's jobs engine Onrec How To Win Your Share Of The Government's £7B Technology Spend Just Like Adzuna Forbes Football fans turned beermat dream into a jobs giant The Times Job search engine Adzuna raises £8M Series C from Smedvig Capital Techcrunch No one likes paying rent. Some might even compare paying rent to helping out your landlord on their mortgage or growing property portfolio. It’s dead money, but we all need a roof over our heads. Read more
                    ❯ Join us on an intriguing journey as we delve into the potential real-world jobs and salaries of Wonka and his eclectic ensemble, from the devious Slugworth to the wise Noodle, exploring what their careers might entail outside the realm of pure imagination. Read more
                    ❯ Analysis of over 1 million job vacancies by Adzuna in October 2023 has revealed the postcode areas where advertised average salaries are highest, and therefore where jobseekers are most likely to pocket the biggest paycheques. Read more

jobs.title: Content Creator, Video Dataleap 📍 San Francisco 💰 $ 80,000 - 150,000 🏢 on-site
jobs.description: This isn't a marketing role. This is a chance to define how an entire category gets talked about. We just raised $3M to build vibe working. The first agentic work platform for tech-forward companies. We have agents running at everything from 2-person startups to 2,000-person unicorns. The product is working. Now we need someone to show the world what's possible. I need someone who can make people feel something when they see our content. Someone who can take a complex AI workflow and make it look obvious. Someone who can be the face of a movement and not just a brand. Own end-to-end content creation: concept, script, film, edit, publish Create short-form video content that cuts through the noise on TikTok, Instagram, LinkedIn, and YouTube Be the on-camera face of Dataleap. Show up. Build trust. Make people want to follow along Turn real customer use cases into stories that make people say "I need this" Explain technical concepts to non-technical people without dumbing it down Capture how we actually work. The energy. The speed. The craft. Make people feel like they're part of something Document the journey. Build content that makes people want to work here as much as they want to use the product Build repeatable content formats that compound over time Test relentlessly. Figure out what works. Double down Future: Also build out long-form content like our podcast series. Take the next step in your career journey

jobs.title: Head of Growth Rosella Brokerage 📍 Austin 💰 $ 100,000 - 140,000 🏢 on-site
jobs.description: Work directly with founders to build and run the operational engine. Some days you're closing deals, other days building workflows, hiring reps, or redesigning processes. Closer who can also build systems — sales and ops in one person Comfortable owning across sales, operations, and growth AI-native insurance — a massive market being rebuilt from scratch Run high-volume sales calls—get on the phone, handle objections, close deals Build lead generation systems across every profitable channel Drive demand gen optimization using sales feedback to improve targeting Own closing and set the standard for call quality and conversion rates Design and implement brokerage operations workflows that scale Build automation for policy administration, renewals, endorsements, compliance Create SOPs and systems that handle rapid customer growth Work with engineering to ship voice AI, workflow tools, and submission automation Own LTV/CAC systems and unit economics across verticals Build metrics and KPI infrastructure—define and instrument what matters Ship experimentation infrastructure with A/B testing and statistical rigor Create attribution systems across voice, email, web, and referral channels Use AI for pattern detection, anomaly detection, and automated insights Translate customer objections into product fixes by end of day Build compliance and risk management systems Lead cross-functional initiatives across intake → submission → service → close Own verticals—develop expertise and playbooks across sectors Take the next step in your career journey
